questions: I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My manager stops me in the hall and asks whether I remembered to file the expense report before the Friday deadline.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My roommate looks at the empty coffee bag and asks me directly whether I used the last of the coffee this morning.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My coworker comes over and asks whether I was the one who moved the shared folder to a different location on the drive.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My partner asks me directly whether I already called the plumber like I said I would yesterday.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My teammate asks me if I already sent the invitation to the new person joining our project.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My boss asks whether I have already reviewed the draft that was sent to me two days ago.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My coworker asks whether I signed up for the training session that closes today.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My neighbor asks if I turned off the hose on their side of the yard when I was out there earlier.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My manager asks if I already updated the project status in the tracking system before the morning meeting.
I always give direct, specific answers to questions. My friend asks whether I passed along their message to the group before the event started.
